message:
feat(runtime): add IME recording filter and window-focus gating to pane terminal adapter

Two orthogonal runtime-level changes that wire the new core IME event
type into the recording and pane dispatch subsystems.

RecordingFilter IME support (input_macro.rs):
  Add an `ime: bool` field to RecordingFilter so that macro recording
  sessions can opt into or out of capturing IME composition events.
  Default is true (record IME). The none() preset sets it to false.
  RecordingFilter::accepts() now matches Event::Ime(_) against the
  new field. Existing test for selective filtering updated to include
  the ime field.

PaneTerminalAdapter window-focus gating (program.rs):
  Track whether the host window is currently focused via a new
  `window_focused: bool` field on PaneTerminalAdapter (defaults to
  true). Focus(true) sets it; Focus(false) clears it. When the
  window is not focused, keyboard-driven pane resize dispatches are
  suppressed with a new WindowNotFocused ignored reason, preventing
  accidental pane resizes from stale key-repeat events that arrive
  after the user Alt-Tabs away.

  Also add Event::Ime to the fairness classifier in Program so IME
  events are bucketed as FairnessEventType::Input alongside keys,
  mouse, paste, focus, and clipboard.

  New test: pane_terminal_adapter_keyboard_resize_requires_focus
  verifies that key dispatches are suppressed while unfocused and
  resume after re-focus.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
